storyAcceptanceCriteria: AC-1: The user story content is not parseable or meaningful; no formal acceptance criteria can be extracted.

testCases.title: Verify system handles undefined or gibberish input gracefully
testCases.description: Attempt to interact with the system using the gibberish string 'af sdfjksldaf' as input to verify the system responds with an appropriate error or validation message rather than crashing or producing unexpected behavior.
preconditions: The application under test is running and accessible.
A text input field or search/command interface is available.
The tester is logged in with a valid test account if authentication is required.
steps: 1. Navigate to the main application page or the relevant input screen.
2. Locate a text input field (e.g., search bar, form field, command input).
3. Type 'af sdfjksldaf' into the input field.
4. Submit the input by pressing Enter or clicking the Submit/Search button.
5. Observe the system response.
testCases.expectedResult: The system should display a meaningful validation or error message (e.g., 'No results found', 'Invalid input', or similar) without crashing, freezing, or exposing sensitive information.
acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: The user story content is not parseable or meaningful; no formal acceptance criteria can be extracted.
testCases.automationReason: Inputting a string and asserting a validation message is a stable, deterministic UI interaction easily handled by Playwright.

testCases.title: Verify system does not crash when receiving random alphanumeric gibberish input
testCases.description: Ensure application stability by submitting random gibberish text to verify no unhandled exceptions, 500 errors, or application crashes occur.
preconditions: The application is deployed and accessible in a test environment.
Browser developer tools are open to monitor console errors and network responses.
steps: 1. Open the application in a supported browser.
2. Open browser developer tools (F12) and navigate to the Console and Network tabs.
3. Locate the primary input or search field on the page.
4. Enter 'af sdfjksldaf' into the field.
5. Submit the input.
6. Check the Console tab for any JavaScript errors or unhandled exceptions.
7. Check the Network tab for any 4xx or 5xx HTTP responses.
8. Observe the UI for any crash indicators, blank screens, or infinite loading states.
testCases.expectedResult: No unhandled JavaScript exceptions appear in the console. No 5xx server errors are returned. The UI remains stable and displays an appropriate user-facing message.
acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: The user story content is not parseable or meaningful; no formal acceptance criteria can be extracted.
testCases.automationReason: Playwright can capture console errors and intercept network responses to assert no crash conditions occur when gibberish input is submitted.

testCases.title: Verify system sanitizes gibberish input to prevent injection attacks
testCases.description: Check that the system properly sanitizes the gibberish input 'af sdfjksldaf' and that it does not expose any SQL injection, XSS, or command injection vulnerabilities when the input is processed.
preconditions: Application is running in a test environment.
Tester has access to a text input that is connected to a backend query or data source.
Security testing tools (e.g., browser dev tools) are available.
steps: 1. Navigate to the application page containing an input field.
2. Enter 'af sdfjksldaf' into the input field.
3. Submit the input.
4. Inspect the network request payload in browser developer tools to confirm the input was transmitted as-is.
5. Verify the response does not include raw database error messages, stack traces, or system information.
6. Confirm the input is not reflected back to the UI in an unsanitized form.
testCases.expectedResult: The system sanitizes the input, no database errors or stack traces are exposed, and no script execution or injection occurs as a result of the input.
acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: The user story content is not parseable or meaningful; no formal acceptance criteria can be extracted.
testCases.automationReason: Security sanitization validation often requires manual inspection of response payloads and server behavior; full security validation typically requires dedicated security tooling beyond Playwright's scope.

testCases.title: Verify user receives a clear and user-friendly error message for unrecognized input
testCases.description: Validate usability by ensuring the error or empty-state message presented to the user for unrecognized input like 'af sdfjksldaf' is clear, polite, and actionable.
preconditions: Tester is on a page with a searchable or queryable input field.
No prior search has been made in the current session.
steps: 1. Navigate to the relevant input screen.
2. Enter 'af sdfjksldaf' into the search or input field.
3. Submit the input.
4. Read the message displayed to the user.
5. Evaluate whether the message clearly communicates the issue.
6. Check if the message provides guidance on what to do next (e.g., try different keywords).
7. Confirm the message uses friendly, non-technical language.
testCases.expectedResult: A clear, user-friendly message is displayed (e.g., 'We couldn't find anything matching your search. Please try different keywords.'). The message is non-technical, readable, and provides actionable guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: The user story content is not parseable or meaningful; no formal acceptance criteria can be extracted.
testCases.automationReason: Usability and tone-of-voice validation of error messages require human judgment and cannot be reliably automated with Playwright alone.

testCases.title: Verify accessibility of the error state triggered by gibberish input
testCases.description: Ensure the error or empty-state message displayed after entering 'af sdfjksldaf' meets basic accessibility standards, including screen reader compatibility and keyboard navigability.
preconditions: Application is running in a test environment.
A screen reader (e.g., NVDA, VoiceOver) is installed and active.
Tester is on the page with the input field.
steps: 1. Open the application using a keyboard-only navigation approach (no mouse).
2. Tab to the input field.
3. Type 'af sdfjksldaf' using the keyboard.
4. Press Enter to submit.
5. Use the screen reader to listen to the announced result or error message.
6. Confirm the error or empty-state message is announced by the screen reader.
7. Verify the error message has appropriate ARIA attributes (e.g., role='alert' or aria-live).
8. Confirm keyboard focus moves to or near the error message after submission.
9. Inspect the DOM for ARIA label or role attributes on the error element.
testCases.expectedResult: The error message is announced by the screen reader immediately upon submission. The message has appropriate ARIA role (e.g., alert) or aria-live attribute. Keyboard focus is placed appropriately so users can understand the outcome without a mouse.
acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: The user story content is not parseable or meaningful; no formal acceptance criteria can be extracted.
testCases.automationReason: Full accessibility validation including screen reader announcements requires manual testing with assistive technologies; automated tools can only partially cover ARIA attribute checks.
